commit.message:
feat(llm): opencode go provider + live model fetch pour Azure/ollama/opencode/mlx

Nouveaux providers et environnements :
  local dev   → PLATFORM_LLM_PROVIDER=local-mlx + LOCAL_MLX_ENABLED=1
                 (Qwen 3.5 35B-A3B-4bit par défaut)
  OVH demo    → PLATFORM_LLM_PROVIDER=opencode + OPENCODE_API_KEY + OPENCODE_BASE_URL
                 (OpenAI-compat Go server, multi-modèles)

provider opencode (client.py) :
  - base_url: OPENCODE_BASE_URL (défaut http://localhost:3000/v1)
  - key: OPENCODE_API_KEY
  - models: vide → fetchés live via /v1/models
  - default: OPENCODE_DEFAULT_MODEL
  - ajouté à _FALLBACK_CHAIN si OPENCODE_API_KEY ou OPENCODE_ENABLED=1

_opencode_enabled dans _FALLBACK_CHAIN :
  - remplace l'ancien hard-code 'openai' qui n'existait pas dans _PROVIDERS

GET /api/llm/providers/{id}/models (api/llm.py) :
  WHY: Azure Foundry, ollama, opencode exposent chacun un endpoint modèles.
  Le polling live évite de hardcoder les déploiements dans le code.
  - azure-ai      → GET /openai/models?api-version=...
  - azure-openai  → GET /openai/deployments?api-version=... (liste les deployments)
  - ollama        → GET /api/tags (liste noms des modèles installés)
  - local-mlx / opencode → GET /v1/models (OpenAI-compat)
  Fallback : retourne la liste statique _PROVIDERS[id][models] si timeout/erreur

settings.html — provider cards :
  - ↻ refresh button sur azure-ai, azure-openai, ollama, local-mlx, opencode
  - Au clic : appelle /api/llm/providers/{id}/models, met à jour _llmModels et
    re-render les selects de routing sans reload de page
  - Couleurs par provider : opencode=violet, local-mlx=jaune, ollama=teal
  - CSS @keyframes spin pour l'animation du bouton refresh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: rewrite GeoPackage writer to use intermediate parquet via hyparquet

Pipeline: DuckDB filters remote parquet → writes intermediate parquet to OPFS →
gpkg_worker reads parquet with hyparquet, derives schema from parquet metadata,
promotes single geometries to MULTI variants, and writes complete GPKG via wa-sqlite.

- Eliminates multiple remote data scans (bbox, geom_type queries)
- Worker is self-contained: reads parquet, creates GPKG schema, inserts rows,
  builds R-tree spatial index, populates metadata
- Hex-encodes WKB in intermediate parquet to avoid hyparquet UTF-8 corruption
- Implements PROMOTE_TO_MULTI at WKB level (matches GDAL behavior)
- Adds hyparquet-compressors for ZSTD support

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(openclaw): update import/export scripts for agents.list format + apply CONSIDERATIONS.md conventions (#5)

`import.sh` and `export.sh` were written against the old `openclaw.json`
structure (`agents` object keyed by `agentId`). The format is now
`agents.list[]` with `id`, and the scripts need to surgically update
only `skills` and `tools` without clobbering other live attributes
(`name`, `model`, etc.).

Additionally, `CONSIDERATIONS.md` contained a set of actionable best
practices that were not yet reflected in agent definitions, coordination
files, or Copilot agent templates.

## Scripts (`import.sh` / `export.sh`)

- `agentId` → `id`; agent lookup changed from object key to `select(.id
== $id)` on the list array
- **Import**: only patches `skills` and `tools` in the workspace file;
all other local attributes are preserved
- **Export**: only patches `skills` and `tools` for the matching list
entry; appends `{id, skills, tools}` for new agents; never overwrites
extra live fields
- Initial bootstrap changed from `{"agents":{}}` to
`{"agents":{"list":[]}}`

```bash
# export: find and patch in-place, append if absent
jq --arg id "$agent_id" --argjson skills "$skills" --argjson tools "$tools" '
  if (.agents.list | map(.id) | index($id)) != null then
    .agents.list = [.agents.list[] | if .id == $id then .skills = $skills | .tools = $tools else . end]
  else
    .agents.list += [{"id": $id, "skills": $skills, "tools": $tools}]
  end
' "$MAIN_CONFIG"
```

## Coordination (`PROTOCOLS.md`)

- **Delegation guardrails**: max session depth = 2 (CEO → PM →
Specialist); no re-delegation; every task must reference a
`vision.md`/`roadmap.md` goal; `sessions_spawn` disabled for specialists
- **Comment signing**: each agent must prefix GitHub comments with its
emoji+name (`📋 [PM Agent]`, `👨‍💻 [dev-backend]`, etc.)
- **Commit author naming**: per-agent `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` convention for
`git log` traceability
- **Label conventions**: `owner:<agent>` and
`type:<feature|bug|research|cost>`; PM keeps them accurate
- **Human-only decisions**: formalised list — merges to main, budget cap
changes, product pivots, auto-merge grants

## Agent definitions

- **CEO `AGENTS.md`**: explicit "never act unilaterally on high-stakes
decisions" + budget-cap guardrail + weekly retrospect trigger in
heartbeat
- **PM `AGENTS.md`**: weekly retrospect response in heartbeat
- **All OpenClaw agents**: comment-signing prefix in Communication
section
- **Biz Research**: "always include sources and confidence levels" +
removed redundant duplicate line
- **Cost Controller**: "never make financial commitments" reinforced

## Copilot agents (`github_copilot_agents/`)

All four agent files now require a structured task-completion comment at
the end of every task:

```
👨‍💻 [dev-backend]
**Files changed:** …
**Tests run:** …
**Suggested follow-up tasks:** …
**Backlog item to update:** …
```

## Docs

- **`README.md`**: rewritten from a one-liner to a full reference
(layout, agent team, import/export, config format, conventions, further
reading)
- **`.github/copilot-instructions.md`**: removed stale `_(planned)_`
markers; added Agent Identification Conventions and Human-Only Decisions
sections; noted that import/export only touches `skills`/`tools`

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->

our [2 minute survey](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-survey).

commit.message:
fix(cockpit): LLM provider status live — chain réelle, pas hardcoded

Avant : tous les 6 providers affichés '✓ OK' dans /cockpit
  - local-mlx/ollama : no_auth=True → has_key=True sans vérifier si le
    serveur tourne (LOCAL_MLX_ENABLED / OLLAMA_ENABLED non testés)
  - azure-ai/azure-openai/nvidia/minimax : clés présentes localement dans
    ~/.config/factory/*.key → tous TRUE même si non configurés en prod

Après : seulement les providers de _FALLBACK_CHAIN ayant une clé réelle
  - local-mlx : has_key = bool(LOCAL_MLX_ENABLED) (défaut False)
  - ollama : has_key = bool(OLLAMA_ENABLED) (défaut False)
  - Autres : vérification inchangée (env var ou fichier .key)
  - Cockpit affiche provider display name + modèle actif + primaire/fallback
  - Ordre : ordre du _FALLBACK_CHAIN, pas ordre alphabétique _PROVIDERS

Exemple prod (PLATFORM_LLM_PROVIDER=azure-openai, AZURE_DEPLOY=1) :
  Azure OpenAI / gpt-5-mini · primaire  ✓ OK
  Azure AI Foundry / gpt-5.2 · fallback ✓ OK

WHY: La transparence sur le provider actif est critique — les agents peuvent
être routés vers un fournisseur différent selon les cooldowns/circuit-breakers.
Le cockpit doit refléter la réalité, pas afficher des faux positifs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: move image upload to shell script for reliable CDI timing

The terraform_data.upload_image resource was not visibly running in
parallel with harvester_image.upload, causing CDI's ~66-second
upload-begin timeout to expire before any data was sent.

New approach for upload mode in run_image_module():
- terraform apply runs in the background (creates CRD, waits for Active)
- _do_upload() runs in the foreground (polls CDI, streams binary, shows
  curl --progress-bar output)
- Once the binary lands the image goes Active and terraform unblocks
- If the upload fails, terraform is killed and the full terraform log
  is shown to help diagnose

Also adds:
- _kc_field(): extracts server URL / bearer token from kubeconfig using
  python3 built-in re (falls back to awk)
- Clear error if kubeconfig uses non-token auth (client certs, exec)
- RetryLimitExceeded detection with cleanup instructions
- terraform_data.upload_image inside the module remains unchanged and
  acts as an idempotent no-op (image already Active) when run via
  harvester-vm.sh, and still handles the concurrent upload case when
  terraform is run directly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[#10195] improvement(catalogs):Change some dependencies to `compileOnly` in catalog modules (#10199)

### What changes were proposed in this pull request?

This pull request refactors dependency configurations across several
catalog modules to reduce unnecessary runtime dependencies. The main
change is switching from `implementation` to `compileOnly` for `:api`,
`:common`, and `:core` dependencies, ensuring these are only available
at compile time and not included in the final runtime artifact. To
maintain test coverage, these dependencies are now explicitly added to
the `testImplementation` scope. This helps minimize the transitive
dependencies included at runtime, leading to lighter builds and fewer
potential conflicts.

### Why are the changes needed?

To shrink the release package size.

Fix: #10195 
Fix: #10250 

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

N/A

### How was this patch tested?

Test locally can CI.

erroneously reported it as still stalled.
Un-stalled completed workflows can now be deleted by the state retention

commit.message:
docs: add metadata-driven architecture requirements to agent instructions (#998)

Agents keep reverting to compiled-type approaches. Add a prominent
METADATA-DRIVEN ARCHITECTURE section to both instruction files making
it explicit that:

- Entity shapes come from metadata, not C# classes
- Field access uses ordinal arrays, not reflection or dictionaries
- UI/validation/routing is gallery-driven, not hard-coded
- A single binary serves any gallery app — only metadata changes

Lists specific anti-patterns to avoid (new entity classes, reflection,
typeof, generic type params for runtime behaviour).

Fixes #996

feat: add ajust task runner (SparkJust for Linux) (#168)

* feat: add ajust task runner (SparkJust for Linux)

- Install just via pacman when sparkfabrik: true
- Deploy ajust justfile to ~/.local/share/ajust/justfile with Linux
  sparkdock_path and sparkdock-fetch-updates recipe
- Install /usr/local/bin/ajust wrapper script
- Imports 05-skills.just from sparkdock (sf-skills-refresh/status)
  and supports ~/.config/ajust/100-custom.just user customizations

* feat: add provision-tags recipe to ajust

Runs ansible-playbook with specified tags, mirroring the Makefile's
local-install-tags target. Provisioner path defaults to ~/provisioner
and can be overridden via AJUST_PROVISIONER_PATH / AJUST_CONFIG_FILE
env vars or ~/.config/ajust/100-custom.just.

* fix: address review findings for ajust

- Replace git stash + reset --hard with git pull --ff-only in
  sparkdock-fetch-updates (safer, no orphaned stashes)
- Add path validation guards to provision-tags recipe (clear error
  messages when provisioner path or config file is missing)
- Align Ansible privilege model: use become_user for user-directory
  tasks, become root only for pacman and /usr/local/bin

* chore: gitignore openspec directory

* refactor: use ~/.local/spark/ajust instead of ~/.local/share/ajust

Keeps ajust alongside sparkdock under the shared ~/.local/spark/ prefix.

* fix: default sparkfabrik to true so sparkdock/ajust tasks run without explicit config

* fix: default sparkfabrik to true in config template

* feat: auto-detect user from SUDO_USER and add ajust zsh completion generation

- Add pre_tasks to system.yml that auto-detect current_user/current_home
  from SUDO_USER (with fallback to ansible_facts), tagged [always] so they
  run regardless of --tags filtering
- Use default(current_user)/default(current_home) fallbacks in sparkdock
  and sparkfabrik roles so username/home config is optional
- Add task to generate ajust zsh completion via just --completions
- Update config template: username/home are now auto-detected, commented
  out with override instructions

* feat: add custom task banner and system import to ajust justfile

Add banner hint for ~/.config/ajust/100-custom.just (matching sjust)

commit.message:
fix: prevent reentrant navigate() calls in followFileIfEnabled

IntelliJ's navigate() pumps EDT events while waiting for tab creation
(blockingWaitForCompositeFileOpen → pumpEventsForHierarchy). This can
dispatch another queued followFileIfEnabled, causing two overlapping
tab insertions that race inside JBTabsImpl.updateText() → NPE.

Add AtomicBoolean guard so only one navigate() executes at a time;
reentrant calls are silently skipped since the user will see the
latest file open anyway.

commit.message:
Wire get_hot_memories() into context retrieval baseline

High-heat memories (heat >= 60) are now appended to raw_memories
after semantic search and plot-critical injection. They act as a
background floor: things the campaign keeps returning to stay in
context even when the current query doesn't semantically match them.

Priority order: semantic results > plot-critical > hot baseline.
Deduped against existing results before append. Capped at 3 entries.
Previously, heat was tracked and decayed every turn but never read.
